basis: Anchor 30 plan lists Kinnan as a GU mana dorks/artifact ramp/combo Commander anchor.
Public Commander and cEDH sources label Kinnan as a Commander/cEDH deck commander.
Kinnan is one of the strongest cEDH signals in Batch A, but casual big-mana play remains distinct.

source_limit_notes: Aggregate/manual reference only; no copied public decklist.
Expected packages exclude banned Mana Crypt, Jeweled Lotus, and Nadu.
Separate casual big-mana creature cheating from cEDH infinite-mana shells.

themes.name: mana_dorks_artifact_ramp_combo
themes.notes: Kinnan doubles nonland mana and turns excess mana into board advantage.

themes.name: infinite_mana_outlets
themes.notes: Monolith and aura loops create deterministic big-mana finishes.

themes.name: simic_creature_cheat
themes.notes: Kinnan's activated ability rewards high-impact creature hits in casual and high-power lanes.

avoid_patterns.reason: Older high-power lists can contain cards banned in Commander.

examples: too many Cultivate effects in cEDH lane
avoid_patterns.reason: Kinnan specifically doubles nonland mana sources.
Some popular dorks have white color identity and are illegal in GU.
